item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed this story, especially the After Action Report from the MTF. I wish more scps included those. I liked the multiple endings but preferred the alternative one.

item.title: HOLY CRAP
item.content:encoded:
This was so awesome! The narrative, the prose, the choice, the writing, all of it!

(Personally, I like the alternative choice the best though, and the Warrior Choice the least. Didn't seem right to erase our cute murder death clone-borg squad like that. At least the godly option implied that they still existed individually)

Alas, I have but one +1 to give for samsara.

item.title: Re: request for page source
item.content:encoded:
Thank you A Random Day.

But still I can't see the source. Even when I access some of offset/0-1, and click "Edit" or "Page Source" at the bottom of each pages, I can only find

(omitted)
[[module ListPages category="fragment" parent="." limit="1" order="created_at" offset="@URL|0"]]
%%content%%
[[/module]]
(omitted)

I also checked a manual page of Listpages Module, and couldn't figure out how to display original source text either.

So if it is not inconvenient for you, please inform me of how to display the original source. I hope you don't mind my request.

item.title: Re: request for page source
item.content:encoded:
All of the source text is available at the following URL:

http://www.scp-wiki.net/samsara/offset/X

where X is replaced with a digit from 0 to 3.

For example:

http://www.scp-wiki.net/samsara/offset/0 leads to the primary page shown here.

item.title: request for page source
item.content:encoded:
Thank you authors for great articles.

I am now planning to translate this article to Japanese. To make things simple, I always use original source and just replace things to Japanese. Because the source is hidden, I would be so happy if I can see it. I look forward to hearing from you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've spotted two typos. A missing space at "ʏᴏᴜʀ ɢɪғᴛs,ʏᴏᴜʀ ʟɪғᴇ" and an extraneous period at "can not just happen! "".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the Alternative ending is really the only one that allows further development at all, the other two were by necessity, pretty final.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The story itself was alright, but I'm not the biggest fan of action sequences in writing. It takes a lot to push past that, and where Avatara did that by seamlessly focusing on building this MTF with narrative and characterization, here it doesn't grab me like it did there. The focus on 2970, an SCP I didn't particularly find myself getting into and one which isn't expanded upon here in a way I enjoyed, sort of did bring down the story a bit for me. I was waiting to get back to the members of Samsara nearly every time that happened.

Prometheus also isn't a group I typically like, but the series has mostly done a good job of making them interesting. Even in the first tale, where their existence is (mostly) downplayed and a secondary element, this seems to bring a lot more of them into the story. I don't particularly care for their role in the story here, and it is mostly because of preference.

And two of the endings (Apotheosis and Ascendancy) don't feel like the most satisfying endings for me. They're both plausible ways for this story to end, but neither struck a chord with me. It was the Alternative ending that I truly enjoyed, and it did wrap up the story in such a neat way and felt satisfying. It's also the one I will take to heart if I decide to visit this MTF in future stories.

Again, I don't think it's necessarily bad. It's certainly enjoyable at times. But it's not for me, and I wasn't that entertained consistently.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know I've said many times in chat that my money's on the Dream Team to win this contest. But that first ending, that was beautiful. The tears in my eyes compel my fingers to give you but one single upvote.

Seriously, that gave me major frisson. Mad props to you, Ty and ARD.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
CYOA? No wonder there were five of this in Newly Created. XD

Man, getting to see the Herald's reckoning with his god was intense.

item.title: Gee wiz! Three whole endings just for me?
item.content:encoded:
Wow. That was a long read. A great read, but long all the same.

As I said when you showed me the draft, I really liked the MTFs dialogue in this. It's so wonderfully awkward, I feel like they keep notes on how words work on their palms. Additionally, the characterization of the mind and the spirit had me routing for both at one point during the tale.

+1. This was a nice conclusion to the series.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, this was awesome.

I was especially impressed that all the pieces worked together to form a coherent arc — and that all three endings felt completely satisfying. Nicely done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy cow. This is amazing.
The end , where you choose the path, is something I have never seen done here before. Groundbreaking. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, that was an appropriately epic ending to a story that got better and better as it went on.

I liked that enough that I think it's earned a retrospective upvote for Avatara.

item.title: And every wikidot module is a source of pain
item.content:encoded:
For those curious about how the formatting on this was done, the following code was used:

[[module ListPages parent="." limit="1" order="created_at" offset="@URL|0"]]
%%content%%
[[/module]]

This was placed on the main samsara page, along with a rating module and a comment telling y'all to keep yer grubby hands off the edit buttons.

The content was then placed on four separate child pages, with wiki page URLs of samsara-1 through samsara-4. These child pages were parented to the main page, and a redirect module was placed at the top of each one, pointing back to the main samsara page.

The code used for the offset links (which allows the List Pages module to load each of the child pages) is simply:

[[[http://www.scp-wiki.net/samsara/offset/X | Link name]]]

Where X is the number of the child page, minus 1. (So offset/0 points to samsara-1 [the main tale body], offset/1 points to samsara-2 [the first ending], etc…

The code for all of this was derived from the usage in SCP-2111 by sirpudding, although it has been modified slightly to suit our usage here.

item.title: Every ending is a new beginning
item.content:encoded:
Tau-5's journey comes to a close… or does it?

Part 6 of a series.

RECOMMENDED READING ORDER:
1. Avatara
2. GRANT REQUEST FOR INVESTIGATING THE APPLICATION OF CERTAIN RESEARCH ASSETS IN OVERCOMING INHERENT LIMITATIONS OF THE HUMAN BODY
3. SCP-2970 (Holy Misplacement)
4. Operation AZURE PEREGRINE
5. The Lord of Endowments
6. Samsara

Huge, huge, huge thanks to everyone that made this possible. Especially my team.

This was a collab effort between me and TyGently (who wrote somewhere between 50-60% of this1).

GreenWolf, our resident technical genius, created the page formatting and offset to make the CYOEndings possible and walked me through the process; he also provided lots of feedback and support.

sirpudding lent his military expertise to the weapons, the names, created poor Rashid Keeper of the Lion Gates, and provided tons of feedback and support.

I'd also like to thank the people who read it and provided feedback plus the folks who helped me when I was brainstorming: Anborough, Jacob Conwell, MacLeod, minmin, Quinlan, Metaphysician, Jekeled, JackIke, Oboebandgeek99, SunnyClockwork, psul, (and probably others).

See more of Ty's work on their author page!
See more of my work on my author page!

Footnotes
1. most likely 50
